description:
# Why

SKILL.md section "Working in a multi-repo hub" tells agents:

  "There is no `repo` field, so pick a convention — a tag
   (`repo:api`, `repo:frontend`) or a stable title prefix"

That is false. `Task` carries `repo: Option<String>` (src/task.rs),
documented in-code as "the code repo this task was created in
(`origin` URL, else repo path). Set at create; legible on a shared
hub." Every task already shows it — `bl show` prints a `repo:` line.

The stale text actively misleads: an agent following it invents a
tag convention to hand-duplicate a field that already exists and is
already populated. The lead sentence "bl does not track which code
repo a ball belongs to" (line 326) is the same error.

# Scope

- Rewrite the "Mark the target repo on the ball" bullet: the `repo`
  field exists, is auto-set at create from the creating clone's
  origin, and is the thing to filter `bl ready` on for per-repo
  triage.
- Soften line 326: bl *does* record an origin repo per ball; the
  honest caveat is that it is create-anchored (see the linked
  provenance ball), not that the field is absent.
- The tag convention is still a fine manual *override* for the
  cross-cutting case — keep it, just stop presenting it as the only
  option.

# Origin

Noticed closing bl-4fbc (the `--resolve-remote` integration test):
the skill guide contradicts the schema. The `repo` field was added
after that SKILL.md section was written.
title: SKILL.md: the 'no repo field' multi-repo guidance is stale
